title: By The Hearth-Stone
content:
By the hearth-stone
    She sits alone,
        The long night bearing:
    With eyes that gleam
    Into the dream
        Of the firelight staring.

    Low and more low
    The dying glow
        Burns in the embers;
    She nothing heeds
    And nothing needs---
        Only remembers.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Henry John Newbolt, Sir delivers a powerful performance in "By The Hearth-Stone"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
